topic: A person finds out their surprise party has been cancelled
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crumpled invitation lay on the kitchen counter, the ink stark and mocking against the cheap paper. A slow, cold burn started low in Elias's stomach and spread outwards, tightening his chest until breathing became a shallow, painful thing. He picked up his phone, his fingers trembling slightly, and dialed the number of the person who had sent the cancellation text. When the voice on the other end confirmed the news, Elias didn't yell. Instead, he placed the phone down with meticulous, almost surgical calm. He walked to the window, stared out at the indifferent city lights, and began methodically cleaning his apartment, every object moved with a sharp, deliberate precision that mirrored the churning resentment inside him. He wasn't going to let this deflate him. He was going to make sure they understood the cost of their carelessness.

[story 2]

"Cancelled." The word echoed in Clara's ears, flat and devoid of warmth. A tremor ran through her hands as she reread the email for the tenth time, each sentence a tiny shard of glass digging into her composure. She felt a strange, hot pressure building behind her eyes, a sensation she usually reserved for extreme frustration. She didn't cry. Instead, she paced the length of her living room, her footsteps heavy and rhythmic on the hardwood floor. She grabbed her coat, not bothering to check her makeup, and headed out. The intention wasn't to mourn the lost celebration; it was to find every single person involved and ensure they felt the exact same hollow ache she was currently experiencing.

[story 3]

Mark stared at the confirmation screen, his jaw clenching so hard a muscle jumped in his cheek. The silence in his small office was deafening, amplifying the frantic hammering of his heart against his ribs. A deep, simmering anger began to coil in his gut, a heavy, dense weight that made him feel physically coiled and ready to spring. He didn't immediately call anyone. He sat there for a full minute, breathing in slow, controlled bursts, gathering the raw energy. When he finally stood up, he didn't smash anything. He simply began rearranging his entire digital archive, deleting, moving, and overwriting files with a furious speed and intensity that spoke volumes about the disappointment he was harboring.
